diagnostic_rule: Use status code class as symptom, not root cause; correlate server/app/proxy logs and request method/path.
risk_notes: HTTP headers can contain tokens/cookies/PII and must be redacted.

diagnostic_rule: Normalize header names by registry; diagnose behavior from semantics, values, intermediaries, and application config.
risk_notes: Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie and proxy credentials must be redacted.

diagnostic_rule: Algorithm mismatch, deprecated algorithms, and policy hardening must be diagnosed using both client and server supported algorithms.
risk_notes: Do not store private keys, full hostnames, user names, or raw authorized_keys without redaction.

diagnostic_rule: TLS alert is a symptom; validate with certificate dates, trust store, SNI, ALPN, protocol version, ciphers, middleboxes, and time sync.
risk_notes: Private keys, client certificates, internal hostnames and PII must be redacted.

diagnostic_rule: DHCP failures require link/VLAN/relay/server/scope/exhaustion context; option code alone is not enough.
risk_notes: MAC addresses and internal network topology should be redacted when stored.

diagnostic_rule: Media type is a declared label; validate actual content via magic bytes and parser behavior before repair/conversion.
risk_notes: Uploaded files can contain sensitive data; store metadata and minimal redacted samples only.

diagnostic_rule: Android build identity must include platform version/API plus vendor firmware/model/region context; build ID alone is insufficient.
risk_notes: Serial numbers, IMEI, Android ID, Google account data and device-owner info must be redacted.
